---
title: "Machine Learning and AI"
description: "Understand AI vocabulary, train simple models, evaluate them responsibly, and know where major libraries fit."
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this chapter matters

Understand AI vocabulary, train simple models, evaluate them responsibly, and know where major libraries fit.

Lessons are now grouped by coherent learner topics. A page may contain multiple
short quizzes when the page has multiple sections, so students can check one
idea before moving to the next.

::: {.questions}
### Questions

- Which lessons belong in this chapter?
- What should I review before moving on?
- How do the lesson sections build one practical skill?
:::

## Lesson path

- [Concepts and Datasets](concepts-and-datasets.qmd) — Distinguish AI concepts and identify features, labels, and datasets.
- [Training and Evaluation](training-and-evaluation.qmd) — Split data, fit a first model, evaluate predictions, and recognize overfitting.
- [Deep Learning Libraries](deep-learning-libraries.qmd) — Understand tensors and where PyTorch and TensorFlow/Keras fit.
- [Transformers Overview](transformers-overview.qmd) — Understand transformer models and why they matter for modern AI.
- [Responsible AI](responsible-ai.qmd) — Use AI tools with attention to privacy, bias, evaluation, and accountability.

## Chapter checkpoint

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this short checkpoint before starting the chapter.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "machine-learning-ai-overview",
  "title": "Machine Learning and AI overview checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "chapter-purpose",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the Machine Learning and AI chapter?",
      "options": [
        "Understand AI vocabulary, train simple models, evaluate them responsibly, and know where major libraries fit.",
        "To memorize disconnected commands",
        "To skip practice",
        "To avoid debugging"
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Understand AI vocabulary, train simple models, evaluate them responsibly, and know where major libraries fit."
    },
    {
      "id": "study-flow",
      "prompt": "Why can a lesson contain multiple quizzes?",
      "options": [
        "Each quiz checks one section before the next section builds on it.",
        "Quizzes replace examples.",
        "Multiple quizzes mean the topic is unrelated.",
        "Quizzes should only appear at the end of the course."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Section-level quizzes help learners review while the idea is still fresh."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include ../_includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
